name: Sam Woodyard
abstract: Sam Woodyard (January 7, 1925 – September 20, 1988) was an American jazz drummer. He was born in Elizabeth, New Jersey, United States.

concerts.label: Duke Ellington in His Concert of Sacred Music

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Clark
about_quotes.evidence: He was using, as his personnel for this record, the Ellington band, everybody except Duke of course... So we had all of us – [Johnny] Hodges, Paul Gonsalves, Jimmy Woode, Sam Woodyard, Jimmy Hamilton. We were all on his date.
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Clark — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Schaap
about_quotes.evidence: I played Milestones at the West End on March 11, 1974 with Paul Quinichette and Matthew Gee, and Sam Woodyard.
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Schaap interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 9, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Lawrence Brown
about_quotes.evidence: Interviewer mentions Sam Woodyard in the context of a tour where Lawrence Brown was playing, but Lawrence Brown does not confirm a personal relationship or direct interaction with Woodyard, only discussing the replacement drummer.

about_quotes.speaker: Ram Ramirez
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Even in later years I always noticed with Duke's band when Sam Woodyard would start to play a shuffle rhythm... It really used to work with that band. [INTERVIEWEE] Oh yeah.

about_quotes.speaker: George Wein
about_quotes.evidence: Jo Jones up there … he wasn't playing, he was standing right next to Sam Woodyard and he had a newspaper and he was banging the newspaper with Sam Woodyard playing the drums.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Wein — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Quentin Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: To me Sam Woodyard was the greatest drummer that Duke has ever had... when Johnny Hodges came back and we didn't have a drummer with us and that's when he got Sam Woodyard
